about:
About TimeNow

TimeNow is a world clock and time tools service that gives you the exact time in any time zone, along with weather, meeting planning, and time-conversion tools. Our goal is to be the clearest, fastest way to see what time it is anywhere on Earth—no account required, no clutter.

Purpose

Whether you're scheduling a call across continents, checking when the sun rises in another city, or simply need the current time in Tokyo or London, TimeNow provides accurate, instant answers. We focus on reliability and readability: large clocks, clear timezone labels, and tools that respect your privacy and your time.

Features

Live world clocks

View the current time in your location and in cities worldwide. Our default grid shows major cities (e.g. New York, London, Paris, Tokyo, Sydney), and you can pin your own cities. Clocks update in real time and reflect the correct local time, including daylight saving where applicable. Each city links to a dedicated page with full time, timezone, and weather information.

Timezone accuracy

We sync with our server so we can report how accurate your device clock is (e.g. “Your clock is accurate to ±20 ms”). Times are shown in the official IANA timezone database, with clear offset (e.g. UTC+1) and DST information, including when the next change occurs. This helps you trust the times you see for meetings and deadlines.

Weather

On the home page and on every city page we show live weather from Open-Meteo: current temperature, “feels like,” conditions, humidity, and wind. You also get a 10-day forecast and a 30-day summary so you can plan trips and events. Weather is cached for a short period to keep the service fast and to reduce external requests.

City and country pages

Thousands of cities have their own pages with local time, sun times, weather, and a map. We also list countries and continents with interactive OpenStreetMap previews so you can explore by region. Where available, we include curated overviews, climate notes, and practical information to help travellers and remote workers.

Time tools

Our tools include: Time difference—see how many hours one timezone is ahead or behind another; Event time in other zones—enter a time and see it in multiple zones; Jet lag planner—get simple guidance for travel across zones; Work hours overlap—find when colleagues in different zones are both “at work”; and Relative time—translate phrases like “tomorrow 9am” into other timezones. These are all run in your browser; we don’t store your inputs.

Meeting and countdown links

Share meeting planner links so others see the same time in two cities, or share countdown links to a specific date and time. Links are stateless: no account, no tracking.

How we build TimeNow

We use open data (e.g. IANA timezones, Open-Meteo weather, OpenStreetMap) and keep the product minimal and fast. We don’t require sign-up; optional preferences (e.g. theme, pinned cities) are stored only in your browser (localStorage). For more on data and privacy, see our Privacy Policy. For questions or feedback, contact us.
privacy:
Privacy Policy

Last updated: March 2025

TimeNow (“we”, “our”, or “the service”) is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains what data we collect, how we use it, and your rights. We aim to be fully transparent and compliant with the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and similar privacy laws.

Data we collect

Minimal data. We do not require you to create an account or to provide your name, email, or any other personal identifier to use TimeNow. We do not collect or store your search queries, selected cities, or timezone preferences on our servers.

Local storage only. To improve your experience, we may store the following only in your browser (localStorage or similar technology that stays on your device):

  • Theme preference (e.g. light or dark mode)
  • Pinned world clocks (city or timezone names you have added)
  • Other UI preferences that you can change or clear at any time

This data never leaves your device unless you clear it yourself. We cannot access it and do not use it to identify you or to track you across sites.

Data we do not collect

We do not collect:

  • Personal data such as name, email, phone number, or address
  • Browsing history, search history, or click streams
  • Persistent identifiers (e.g. advertising IDs) for tracking
  • Location data beyond what your browser may send as part of standard requests (e.g. for timezone detection), which we do not log or store

Server and technical data

When you request a page or an API (e.g. time sync, weather), our server may receive standard technical data such as IP address, user-agent, and referrer. We may use this only to operate and secure the service (e.g. rate limiting, abuse prevention) and, if we use analytics, in aggregated form only. We do not use this data to build profiles about you. Where we use third-party services (e.g. hosting), we ensure they process data in line with this policy and applicable law.

Cookies

We do not set cookies for tracking or advertising. If we ever use strictly necessary cookies (e.g. for security or load balancing), we will describe them here and, where required, obtain consent.

Your rights (GDPR)

If you are in the European Economic Area or the UK, you have the right to:

  • Access any personal data we hold about you (we hold minimal or none)
  • Rectification of inaccurate data
  • Erasure (“right to be forgotten”)
  • Restrict or object to processing
  • Data portability, where applicable
  • Lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority

To exercise these rights or to ask questions about our use of data, please contact us. We will respond within a reasonable time and in line with legal requirements.

Children

TimeNow is not directed at children under 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe we have received data from a child, please contact us and we will delete it.

Changes

We may update this policy from time to time. The “Last updated” date at the top will change when we do. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy. We encourage you to review this page periodically.
terms:
Terms of Service

Last updated: March 2025

Please read these Terms of Service (“Terms”) before using TimeNow (“the service”, “we”, “our”). By using the service, you agree to these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use the service.

Use of the service

TimeNow provides world clock, timezone, weather, and related time tools for personal and informational use. You may use the service only in compliance with these Terms and with all applicable laws. You may not use the service to harm others, to attempt to gain unauthorized access to our or any third-party systems, or to transmit malware or spam. You may not scrape, crawl, or automate access in a way that overloads or disrupts the service.

Accuracy and “as is”

We strive to show accurate time and timezone information based on the IANA timezone database and server sync. Weather data comes from third-party providers (e.g. Open-Meteo) and we do not guarantee its accuracy or availability. All content and tools are provided “as is” and “as available” without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied, including but not limited to implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement. We do not warrant that the service will be uninterrupted, error-free, or free of harmful components.

No professional advice

TimeNow is for general information only. It is not legal, financial, medical, or travel advice. Do not rely on it for critical decisions (e.g. travel, contracts, or deadlines) without verifying times and rules with official sources. We are not liable for any loss or damage arising from your use of or reliance on the service.

Limitation of liability

To the fullest extent permitted by law, TimeNow and its operators shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or for any loss of profits, data, or goodwill, arising from your use or inability to use the service. In no event shall our total liability exceed the amount you paid to us in the twelve months preceding the claim (or, if nothing was paid, zero). Some jurisdictions do not allow certain limitations; in those jurisdictions, our liability will be limited to the maximum extent permitted by law.

Third-party content and links

The service may include content, data, or links from third parties (e.g. maps, weather). We do not control and are not responsible for third-party content, privacy practices, or terms. Your use of third-party services is at your own risk.

Intellectual property

TimeNow’s branding, layout, and original content are owned by us or our licensors. You may not copy, modify, or distribute them without permission. You may link to our public pages in a fair and non-misleading way.

Termination

We may suspend or discontinue the service or your access at any time, with or without notice, for any reason. Upon termination, your right to use the service ceases. Sections that by their nature should survive (e.g. disclaimers, limitation of liability) will survive.

Changes to the Terms

We may update these Terms. The “Last updated” date will change when we do. Continued use after changes means you accept the new Terms. We encourage you to review this page periodically.

Contact

For questions about these Terms, please contact us.
contact:
Contact

We’d like to hear from you. Use the options below for questions, feedback, or reports (e.g. incorrect timezone or broken links).

Email

Send an email to: hello@timenow.example.com

We aim to reply within a few business days. For privacy or data requests (e.g. access, erasure), please use the same address and put “Privacy” or “Data request” in the subject line so we can route it correctly and respond within the timeframes required by law.

What to include

  • A short description of your question or issue
  • If reporting a bug: your browser and device (e.g. Chrome on Windows), and the page or feature (e.g. “city page for London”)
  • If you are contacting us about privacy or data: your country or region so we can apply the right rules

We do not support unsolicited commercial messages. For other inquiries, go back to the home page or read our About and Privacy Policy.
